mappings.rationale: Palmer's VSI is directly focused on the atmosphere, covering its composition, thermal structure, upper atmospheric phenomena, and large-scale atmospheric motions.
Maslin covers atmospheric composition changes including the carbon budget, greenhouse gases, and their effects on the atmosphere's thermal structure and circulation.
Directly relevant to atmospheric circulation patterns, jet streams, trade winds, monsoons, and the large-scale motions of the atmosphere discussed in this section.
Dunlop covers atmospheric structure, wind systems, and the physical processes driving atmospheric motions including cyclones, anticyclones, and jet streams.
Lenton discusses the atmosphere as a component of the Earth system, including its chemical cycles (carbon, nitrogen, sulfur budgets) and interactions with other spheres.
Redfern covers the Earth's atmosphere in the context of the planet's overall structure, including atmospheric layers and their properties.
McGuire discusses atmospheric hazards including hurricanes, typhoons, and severe weather systems related to the large-scale atmospheric motions covered in this section.
Lowrie covers the ionosphere, Van Allen radiation belts, and the upper atmosphere's interaction with the Earth's magnetic field—topics addressed in this section.
Judge covers solar radiation and solar-terrestrial interactions relevant to ionospheric disturbances of solar origin, auroras, and magnetic storms discussed in this section.
Attfield addresses the ethical dimensions of atmospheric pollution and ozone depletion, relevant to the section's discussion of atmospheric composition changes.
